prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetheart, well... from what I understand, the CPU is sort of like the *brain* of the computer. They call it the "Central Processing Unit," which sounds very fancy, but really it's the little part inside that does all the thinking and figures things out when you click on something or type a letter. Your grandfather used to say it's a bit like the engine in a car — you can't really see it doing its work, but nothing would go anywhere without it. I think it's just a tiny little square chip, if you can believe that! Imagine, something smaller than a cracker doing all that math and whatnot. Now, if you want to know any more than that, you'd better ask your cousin Jeremy, because that's about the end of what this old noggin knows!
